negative_space.remaining_ordinary_error_band: All other tested tids in 456-521 returned the ordinary -111005 error branch on the current 3-sample anonymous sweep.

current_best_reading: API1 tid=453 is not an isolated anonymous anomaly. The broader 456-521 corridor contains additional stable API1 noncanonical success-shell families at 476, 483, and 506, but they are all thinner than canonical 431 and thinner in different ways.

API2 positive tid space is no longer just 535/540/541 in the anonymous tested map. The 456-521 corridor adds stable alternate positive branches at 488 and 502, plus a stable success-shell family at 506.
The new 490 and 514 islands strengthen the idea that mid-band anonymous tid space contains both positive shells and forbidden-external local islands, not just a flat ordinary-error band.

API2 tid=506 should currently be read conservatively as a success-shell family rather than a proved richer positive branch, because the current primary live fetch still exposed no non-empty XML or JSONP fields despite top-level success.
follow_up_priority: Promote API2 tid=488 and tid=502 into a dedicated capability-delta comparison against 535/540/541 before caller-facing tables are widened.
Promote API1 tid=476/483/506 into an API1 shell-family comparison against 431/453/537 before treating them as direct-call recipes.
Keep 490/514 as stable forbidden-external islands and avoid treating nearby ordinary -111005 tids as positive leads without a new branch signal.
